description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, flat stone slab with a reddish-brown coloration and a rough, natural texture. The surface is uneven, featuring both pitted and slightly wavy areas, with some visible indentations and a few darker patches. One side of the stone displays a series of shallow, parallel grooves, likely formed by natural weathering or sedimentary layering. There are handwritten markings in black ink on two sides, suggesting it may have been cataloged or studied. The edges are jagged and asymmetrical, giving the stone a rugged, unrefined appearance. Its overall form and size suggest it could serve as a small barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or garden path.
description: A flat, reddish-brown stone slab with a rough, uneven surface and jagged edges, featuring some handwritten markings and natural grooves.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing its reddish-brown color, rough texture, and handwritten markings near one edge.
description_view_1: A thin, side profile reveals the stone's flatness and irregular, jagged edge contours.
description_view_2: The reverse side displays pronounced wavy grooves and a more textured, uneven surface.
description_view_3: Another broad face is shown, similar to view 0 but with different markings and a slightly more eroded appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, natural grooves and pitting with some parallel wavy lines; no artificial carvings but visible handwritten markings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible pitting and grooves, indicating moderate exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil or gravel, may contrast with concrete or manicured landscaping; could be partially embedded or surrounded by vegetation.
